name: DeepDiff (Deep Difference and Search of Python Objects)

performance.notes: Recursive comparison; performance proportional to data size and depth

agent_readiness.notes: Deep difference and search for Python objects. from deepdiff import DeepDiff, DeepSearch, DeepHash. diff = DeepDiff(t1, t2). diff['values_changed']. diff['type_changes']. diff['dictionary_item_added']. diff['iterable_item_added']. Ignore order: DeepDiff(t1, t2, ignore_order=True). Significant digits: DeepDiff(t1, t2, significant_digits=2). Exclude paths: exclude_paths={'root.name'}. DeepSearch(obj, item, verbose_level=2). DeepHash(obj) for hash of nested structure. JSON output: diff.to_json(). Patch: delta = Delta(diff); t1 + delta = t2.

gotchas: Large deeply nested structures can be slow to compare
Output format can be verbose for large diffs
ignore_order on large lists is O(n²)
Custom operators needed for domain-specific equality
best_when: Comparing API responses in tests to detect unexpected changes
Test assertions for complex nested data structures
avoid_when: Performance-critical loops comparing large structures frequently
summary: DeepDiff compares nested Python objects and dicts. DeepDiff(t1, t2) returns structured diff with values_changed, dictionary_item_added, type_changes. ignore_order=True for unordered comparison. DeepSearch(obj, item) to find values. DeepHash for hashing nested structures. Delta for applying diffs. MIT. Type annotations. Best for test assertions on complex data structures and API response drift detection.
